page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.6xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ecs.u2a family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor operating at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The instance is configured with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated compute resources without hyper-threading.

This server is equipped with 48.0 GB of memory, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 7 Gbps.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is structured for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation offers predictable performance for multi-threaded applications, batch processing, and compute-intensive tasks. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads relying entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound calculations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.6xlarge is a compute-optimized x86_64 server instance powered by a 2.25 GHz AMD EPYC processor. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it provides 24 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration. The instance features 48.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps. This resource profile is designed for compute-intensive workloads, batch processing, and multi-threaded applications that require dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of hyper-threading or the need for large memory capacities.
